Container.Name: travstats-db
Container.Overview:
PostgreSQL 15 with the PostGIS 3.4 spatial extension — the database TravStats expects.
This template is pre-filled with the container name `travstats-db` and the database/user pair `flights/flights` so the default `DATABASE_URL` in the TravStats template matches without further editing. **Change the password to a strong value** (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 32`) and reuse it in TravStats.
**Install order:** this container **first**, then the TravStats template. The database is published on the Unraid host at port 5432 (adjustable below) so TravStats can reach it via `host.docker.internal:5432` without needing a shared Docker network. Keep the port free of conflicts with other PostgreSQL containers you may already run.
**Storage:** the default Application Data path (`/mnt/user/appdata/travstats-db`) maps to PostgreSQL's data directory. The initial cluster is created on first start and then kept in place on updates; deleting that directory wipes the database, so point your backup tool at it.
**Why PostGIS, not plain Postgres?** TravStats uses PostGIS for airport and route geometry. Plain `postgres:15` fails the first TravStats migration.
Container.Requires: Used together with the TravStats Community Apps template.

Config.Name: Application Data
Config.Description: PostgreSQL data directory — one persistent mount, survives container updates. Back this path up with your regular appdata backup.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Database password. Generate a strong one with `openssl rand -base64 32`, then paste the same value into the TravStats DATABASE_URL field.

Config.Name: Host Port
Config.Description: Port on the Unraid host that PostgreSQL listens on. TravStats connects through host.docker.internal:<this port>. Change only if 5432 is already in use by another Postgres.
